packet.raw_text:
{
  "changed_files": [
    "index.html",
    "styles.css"
  ],
  "summary": [
    "Lightened the homepage visual treatment by simplifying backgrounds, softening shadows, and keeping the existing hero, about, motto, posts, and contact sections intact.",
    "Improved responsive readability with tighter spacing rules, cleaner card styling, better small-screen navigation behavior, and full-width mobile buttons.",
    "Added lightweight accessibility improvements in the HTML, including section heading associations, a theme color meta tag, and descriptive aria-labels for repeated post links."
  ],
  "verification": [
    "Ran `git diff -- index.html styles.css` to inspect the final patch.",
    "Ran `python -c \"from html.parser import HTMLParser ...\"` against `index.html` and confirmed `html_parse_ok`.",
    "Ran a Python check to confirm `hero-title`, `about-title`, `motto-title`, `posts-title`, and `contact-title` IDs exist: `missing_ids=none`.",
    "Ran a CSS presence check for reduced-motion/mobile/focus-related rules and got `css_checks=ok`."
  ],
  "open_issues": [
    "Browser-side visual QA was not performed in this CLI environment."
  ],
  "risks": [],
  "next_step": "Open the static page in a browser and do a quick desktop/mobile pass to confirm spacing, contrast, and tap targets feel right."
}

summary: Lightened the homepage visual treatment by simplifying backgrounds, softening shadows, and keeping the existing hero, about, motto, posts, and contact sections intact.
Improved responsive readability with tighter spacing rules, cleaner card styling, better small-screen navigation behavior, and full-width mobile buttons.
Added lightweight accessibility improvements in the HTML, including section heading associations, a theme color meta tag, and descriptive aria-labels for repeated post links.
verification: Ran `git diff -- index.html styles.css` to inspect the final patch.
Ran `python -c "from html.parser import HTMLParser ..."` against `index.html` and confirmed `html_parse_ok`.
Ran a Python check to confirm `hero-title`, `about-title`, `motto-title`, `posts-title`, and `contact-title` IDs exist: `missing_ids=none`.
Ran a CSS presence check for reduced-motion/mobile/focus-related rules and got `css_checks=ok`.
open_issues: Browser-side visual QA was not performed in this CLI environment.
parsed.next_step: Open the static page in a browser and do a quick desktop/mobile pass to confirm spacing, contrast, and tap targets feel right.

packet.raw_text:
{
  "current_goal": "Complete one focused beautification and lightweight optimization pass for the static personal blog homepage while preserving content and structure.",
  "completed": [
    "Inspected the existing `index.html` and `styles.css` structure.",
    "Updated the HTML with accessibility-focused labels and more descriptive repeated links.",
    "Reworked the CSS toward a lighter, less visually heavy presentation with improved mobile readability.",
    "Ran basic markup and stylesheet verification checks."
  ],
  "key_files": [
    "index.html",
    "styles.css"
  ],
  "latest_verification": [
    "`git diff -- index.html styles.css` reviewed cleanly.",
    "HTML parser check returned `html_parse_ok`.",
    "ID presence check returned `missing_ids=none`.",
    "CSS rule presence check returned `css_checks=ok`."
  ],
  "open_questions": [],
  "risks": [],
  "suggested_next_step": "Perform a browser render check at desktop and mobile widths, then decide whether to stop here or do one smaller polish pass.",
  "web_recovery_prompt": "Open `index.html` in a browser and review the page at roughly 1280px and 390px widths. Check that the hero feels lighter than before, cards remain readable, nav links and buttons are easy to tap on mobile, focus states are visible, and the preserved section structure/content still reads naturally. If anything looks off, report the specific spacing, contrast, or wrapping issue and continue with a minimal CSS-only adjustment."
}

webLoop.webReplyRaw:
```context-packet
project_id: personal_blog_smoke
cycle_id: 021
status: ready
inherits_from:
- cycle_id: 020
focus: Add a skip-to-content link with matching focus-visible styling for faster keyboard navigation.
reason:
- It is a small, practical accessibility improvement that fits the current static homepage.
- It builds directly on the previous cycle's lighter styling and accessibility pass without changing content structure.
acceptance_criteria:
- The first focusable element on the page is a skip link.
- The skip link targets the main content container via a valid ID.
- The skip link stays visually hidden until focused, then becomes clearly visible.
- The change works cleanly at desktop and mobile widths without disturbing the current layout.
key_files:
- index.html
- styles.css
implementation_prompt: Add a `Skip to content` link near the top of `index.html` that points to the main content wrapper, ensure the target element has a stable ID, and add CSS in `styles.css` so the link is off-screen by default but clearly visible on keyboard focus. Keep the styling consistent with the lighter homepage treatment and avoid broader visual changes. Verify the target ID exists and that the focused skip link state is present in the stylesheet.
```
